heading: Research and development
label: 1522 Research and development
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Amendments2025—Subsec. (c)(7). struck out subpar. (F). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Notwithstanding , with respect to plans described under this paragraph, the term ‘beginning farmer or rancher’ means a farmer or rancher who has not actively operated and managed a farm or ranch with a bona fide insurable interest in a crop or livestock as an owner-operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper for more than 10 crop years.”Pub. L. 119–21section 1502(b)(3) of this title2018—Subsec. (b)(1)(B). , amended subpar. (B) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “An applicant who submits a policy under shall be eligible for the reimbursement of reasonable research and development costs directly related to the policy if the policy is approved by the Board for sale to producers.”Pub. children.heading: Definition of policy
children.label: a Definition of policy
children.text: In this section, the term “policy” means a policy, plan of insurance, provision of a policy or plan of insurance, and related materials.

children.heading: Reimbursement of research, development, and maintenance costs
children.label: b Reimbursement of research, development, and maintenance costs

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall provide a payment to an applicant for research and development costs in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An applicant who submits a policy under shall be eligible for the reimbursement of reasonable research and development costs if the policy is approved by the Board for sale to producers.section 1508(h) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: for any employees or contracted personnel, wage rates equal to not more than 2 times the hourly wage rate plus benefits, as provided by the Bureau of Labor Statistics for the year in which such costs are incurred, calculated using the formula applied to an applicant by the Corporation in reviewing proposed project budgets under this section on ; andOctober 1, 2016

children.label: II
children.text: other actual documented costs incurred by the applicant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the other provisions of this paragraph, the Board may approve the request of an applicant for advance payment of a portion of reasonable research and development costs prior to submission and approval of the policy by the Board under .section 1508(h) of this title

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: B Procedures
children.text: The Board shall establish procedures for approving advance payment of reasonable research and development costs to applicants.

children.label: i
children.text: a summary of the qualifications of the applicant, including any prior concept proposals and submissions to the Board under and, if applicable, any work conducted under this section;section 1508(h) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a projection of total research and development costs that the applicant expects to incur;

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the need for the policy, the marketability of and expected demand for the policy among affected producers, and the potential impact of the policy on producers and the crop insurance delivery system;

children.label: iv
children.text: a summary of data sources available to demonstrate that the policy can reasonably be developed and actuarially appropriate rates established; and

children.label: v
children.text: an identification of the risks the proposed policy will cover and an explanation of how the identified risks are insurable under this subchapter.

children.heading: Experts
children.label: i Experts
children.text: If the requirements of subparagraph (B) and (C) are met, the Board may submit a concept proposal described in subparagraph (C) to not less than 2 independent expert reviewers, whose services are appropriate for the type of concept proposal submitted, to assess the likelihood that the proposed policy being developed will result in a viable and marketable policy, as determined by the Board.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: ii Timing
children.text: The time frames described in subparagraphs (C) and (D) of shall apply to the review of concept proposals under this subparagraph.section 1508(h)(4) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the concept, in good faith, will likely result in a viable and marketable policy consistent with ;section 1508(h) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: in a significantly improved form;

children.label: bb
children.text: to a crop or region not traditionally served by the Federal crop insurance program; or

children.label: cc
children.text: in a form that addresses a recognized flaw or problem in the program;

children.label: III
children.text: the applicant agrees to provide such reports as the Corporation determines are necessary to monitor the development effort;

children.label: IV
children.text: the proposed budget and timetable are reasonable, as determined by the Board; and

children.label: V
children.text: the concept proposal meets any other requirements that the Board determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: the intended policy or plan of insurance developed by the submitter will provide coverage for a region or crop that is underserved by the Federal crop insurance program, including specialty crops; and

children.label: II
children.text: the submitter is making satisfactory progress towards developing a viable and marketable policy or plan of insurance consistent with .section 1508(h) of this title

children.heading: Submission of policy
children.label: F Submission of policy
children.text: If the Board approves an advanced payment under subparagraph (E), the Board shall establish a date by which the applicant shall present a submission in compliance with (including the procedures implemented under that section) to the Board for approval.section 1508(h) of this title

children.heading: Approved policies
children.label: i Approved policies
children.text: If a policy is submitted under subparagraph (F) and approved by the Board under and the procedures established by the Board (including procedures established under subparagraph (B)), the applicant shall be eligible for a payment of reasonable research and development costs in the same manner as policies reimbursed under paragraph (1)(B), less any payments made pursuant to subparagraph (E).section 1508(h) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: not seek a refund of any payments made in accordance with this paragraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: not make any further research and development cost payments associated with the submission of the policy under this paragraph.

children.heading: Policy not submitted
children.label: H Policy not submitted
children.text: If an applicant receives an advance payment and fails to fulfill the obligation of the applicant to the Board by not submitting a completed submission without just cause and in accordance with the procedures established under subparagraph (B)) , including notice and reasonable opportunity to respond, as determined by the Board, the applicant shall return to the Board the amount of the advance plus interest.1So in original. The second closing parenthesis probably should not appear.1

children.label: i
children.text: a concept proposal or submission that did not result in a marketable product; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a concept proposal or submission of poor quality.

children.heading: Continued eligibility
children.label: J Continued eligibility
children.text: A determination that an applicant is not eligible for advance payments under this paragraph shall not prevent an applicant from reimbursement under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Waiver for hemp
children.label: K Waiver for hemp
children.text: The Board may waive the viability and marketability requirements under this paragraph in the case of research and development relating to a policy to insure the production of hemp.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Corporation shall approve a reimbursement under paragraph (1) only after determining that the policy is marketable based on a reasonable marketing plan, as determined by the Board.

children.heading: Waiver for hemp
children.label: B Waiver for hemp
children.text: The Corporation may waive the marketability requirement under subparagraph (A) in the case of research and development relating to a policy to insure the production of hemp.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: A Requirement
children.text: The Corporation shall reimburse maintenance costs associated with the annual cost of underwriting for a policy described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Duration
children.label: B Duration
children.text: Payments with respect to maintenance costs may be provided for a period of not more than four reinsurance years subsequent to Board approval for payment under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: maintain the policy and charge a fee to approved insurance providers that elect to sell the policy under this subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: transfer responsibility for maintenance of the policy to the Corporation.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: i Amount
children.text: Subject to approval by the Board, the amount of the fee that is payable by an approved insurance provider that elects to sell the policy shall be an amount that is determined by the applicant maintaining the policy.

children.label: I
children.text: is unreasonable in relation to the maintenance costs associated with the policy; or

children.label: II
children.text: unnecessarily inhibits the use of the policy.

children.label: I
children.text: the applicant petitions the Board for reconsideration of the fee;

children.label: II
children.text: a substantial change is made to the policy, as determined by the Board; or

children.label: III
children.text: there is substantial evidence that the fee is inhibiting sales or use of the policy, as determined by the Board.

children.heading: Treatment of payment
children.label: 5 Treatment of payment
children.text: Payments made under this subsection for a policy shall be considered as payment in full by the Corporation for the research and development conducted with regard to the policy and any property rights to the policy.

children.heading: Reimbursement amount
children.label: 6 Reimbursement amount
children.text: The Corporation shall determine the amount of the payment under this subsection for an approved policy based on the complexity of the policy and the size of the area in which the policy or material is expected to be sold.

children.label: i
children.text: there is traditionally, and continues to be, a low level of Federal crop insurance participation and availability; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the State is underserved by the Federal crop insurance program;

children.label: B
children.text: increase participation in areas that are underserved by the Federal crop insurance program; and

children.label: C
children.text: increase participation by producers of underserved agricultural commodities, including specialty crops.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: The Corporation may conduct research and development or enter into contracts under procedures prescribed by the Corporation with qualified persons to carry out research and development for policies that promote the purposes of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: Before conducting research and development or entering into a contract under subparagraph (A), the Corporation shall consult with groups representing producers of agricultural commodities that would be served by the policies that are the subject of the research and development.

children.heading: Qualified persons
children.label: 3 Qualified persons
children.text: A person with experience in crop insurance or farm or ranch risk management (including a college or university, an approved insurance provider, and a trade or research organization), as determined by the Corporation, shall be eligible to enter into a contract with the Corporation under this subsection.

children.heading: Types of contracts
children.label: 4 Types of contracts
children.text: A contract under this subsection may provide for research and development regarding new or expanded policies, including policies based on adjusted gross income, cost-of-production, quality losses, and an intermediate base program with a higher coverage and cost than catastrophic risk protection.

children.heading: Use of resulting policies
children.label: 5 Use of resulting policies
children.text: The Corporation may offer any policy developed under this subsection that is approved by the Board after expert review in accordance with .section 1505(e) of this title

children.heading: Research and development priorities
children.label: 6 Research and development priorities
children.text: The Corporation shall establish as one of the highest research and development priorities of the Corporation the development of policies that increase participation by producers of underserved agricultural commodities, including sweet sorghum, biomass sorghum, rice, peanuts, sugarcane, alfalfa, pennycress, dedicated energy crops, and specialty crops.

children.heading: Whole farm diversified risk management insurance plan
children.label: 7 Whole farm diversified risk management insurance plan

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless the Corporation approves a whole farm insurance plan, similar to the plan described in this paragraph, to be available to producers for the 2016 reinsurance year, the Corporation shall conduct activities or enter into contracts to carry out research and development to develop a whole farm risk management insurance plan, with a liability limitation of $1,500,000, that allows a diversified crop or livestock producer the option to qualify for an indemnity if actual gross farm revenue is below 85 percent of the average gross farm revenue or the expected gross farm revenue that can reasonably be expected of the producer, as determined by the Corporation.

children.heading: Eligible producers
children.label: B Eligible producers
children.text: The Corporation shall permit producers (including direct-to-consumer marketers and producers servicing local and regional and farm identity-preserved markets) who produce multiple agricultural commodities, including specialty crops, industrial crops, livestock, and aquaculture products, to participate in the plan developed under subparagraph (A) in lieu of any other plan under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: grow multiple crops; or

children.label: ii
children.text: may have income from the production of livestock that uses a crop grown on the farm.

children.heading: Market readiness
children.label: D Market readiness
children.text: The Corporation may include coverage for the value of any packing, packaging, or any other similar on-farm activity the Corporation determines to be the minimum required in order to remove the commodity from the field.

children.heading: Review of modifications to improve effectiveness
children.label: E Review of modifications to improve effectiveness
I
children.text: the Corporation shall hold stakeholder meetings to solicit producer and agent feedback; and

children.label: aa
children.text: review procedures and paperwork requirements on agents and producers; and

children.label: bb
children.text: modify procedures and requirements, as appropriate, to decrease burdens and increase flexibility and effectiveness.

children.label: I
children.text: removing caps on nursery and livestock production;

children.label: II
children.text: allowing a waiver to expand operations, especially for small and beginning farmers;

children.label: III
children.text: minimizing paperwork for producers and agents;

children.label: IV
children.text: implementing an option for producers with less than $1,000,000 in gross revenue that requires significantly less paperwork and recordkeeping;

children.label: V
children.text: developing and using alternative records such as time-stamped photographs or technology applications to document planting and production history;

children.label: VI
children.text: treating the different growth stages of aquaculture species as separate crops to recognize the difference in perils at different phases of growth;

children.label: aa
children.text: using an average of the historic and projected revenue;

children.label: bb

children.label: cc
children.text: counting payments under as historic revenue for loss years; orsection 7333 of this title

children.label: VIII

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall carry out research and development, or offer to enter into 1 or more contracts with 1 or more qualified persons to carry out research and development, regarding the creation of a separate practice for subsurface irrigation, including the establishment of a separate transitional yield within a county that is reflective of the average gain in productivity and yield associated with the installation of a subsurface irrigation system.

children.label: 19 High-risk, highly productive batture land policy
I

children.heading: Partnerships for risk management development and implementation
children.label: d Partnerships for risk management development and implementation
A
children.text: increasing the availability of loss mitigation, financial, and other risk management tools for producers, with a priority given to risk management tools for producers of agricultural commodities covered by , specialty crops, and underserved agricultural commodities; orsection 7333 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: improving analysis tools and technology regarding compliance or identifying and using innovative compliance strategies.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 2 Authority
children.text: The Corporation may enter into partnerships with the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, the Agricultural Research Service, the National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration, and other appropriate public and private entities with demonstrated capabilities in developing and implementing risk management and marketing options for producers of specialty crops and underserved agricultural commodities.

children.label: A
children.text: to enhance the notice and timeliness of notice of weather conditions that could negatively affect crop yields, quality, and final product use in order to allow producers to take preventive actions to increase end product profitability and marketability and to reduce the possibility of crop insurance claims;

children.label: B
children.text: to develop a multifaceted approach to pest management and fertilization to decrease inputs, decrease environmental exposure, and increase application efficiency;

children.label: C
children.text: to develop or improve techniques for planning, breeding, planting, growing, maintaining, harvesting, storing, shipping, and marketing that will address quality and quantity challenges associated with year-to-year and regional variations;

children.label: D
children.text: to clarify labor requirements and assist producers in complying with requirements to better meet the physically intense and time-compressed planting, tending, and harvesting requirements associated with the production of specialty crops and underserved agricultural commodities;

children.label: E
children.text: to provide assistance to State foresters or equivalent officials for the prescribed use of burning on private forest land for the prevention, control, and suppression of fire;

children.label: F
children.text: to provide producers with training and informational opportunities so that the producers will be better able to use financial management, farm financial benchmarking, crop insurance, marketing contracts, and other existing and emerging risk management tools;

children.label: G
children.text: to improve analysis tools and technology regarding compliance or identifying and using innovative compliance strategies; and

children.label: H
children.text: to develop other risk management tools to further increase economic and production stability.

children.heading: Reimbursements
children.label: 1 Reimbursements
children.text: Of the amounts made available from the insurance fund established under , the Corporation may use to provide reimbursements under subsection (b) not more than $7,500,000 for fiscal year 2008 and each subsequent fiscal year.section 1516(c) of this title

children.heading: Conducting and contracting for research and development
children.label: A Conducting and contracting for research and development
i
children.text: $12,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2018; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $8,000,000 for fiscal year 2019 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.heading: Underserved States
children.label: B Underserved States
children.text: Of the amount made available under subparagraph (A) for a fiscal year, the Corporation shall use not more than $5,000,000 for the fiscal year to conduct research and development and carry out contracting for research and development to carry out the purpose described in subsection (c)(1)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: increasing compliance-related training;

children.label: ii
children.text: improving analysis tools and technology regarding compliance;

children.label: iii
children.text: use of information technology, as determined by the Corporation; and

children.label: iv
children.text: identifying and using innovative compliance strategies; and

children.label: B
children.text: any excess amounts to carry out other activities authorized under this section.
